- The view-local 90-day upcomingEventsLong is the correct move in all three: the shared 14-day upcomingEvents (Dashboard.tsx 867-873) feeds other widgets and must stay capped.
- B's one extra dependency is provably cheap, not speculative: onAdvanceStage already exists (Dashboard.tsx:798) and threads done-state via completedAt (line 808). An additive optional ctx.onCompleteItem(topicId,itemId) is a near-copy of that pattern, so older call sites stay valid and there's zero schema/auth/CI touch.
- B also correctly pre-identifies the only real edge case: calendar-event entries use synthetic ids (eventItemId, util.ts:86) with no completable backing item and section 'calendar' (line 94), so they show a "view in calendar" affordance instead of a checkbox. That's a handled detail, not a hidden landmine.

Net: B is the most differentiated, most reopen-worthy variant whose headline interaction is backed by an existing, trivially-extended completion path. Bold per dogfood-v2, but grounded.

experiments.priority: Make day-one momentum real: multi-select cold-start that seeds her whole life + an always-on 'Add a deadline...' bar at the top of the Briefing

variants.name: Multi-Pick Setup + Pinned Briefing Bar
variants.approach: The faithful, lowest-risk realization of the two fused gaps. COLD-START: convert WelcomeColdStart to multi-select. Replace the single `hover` state with a `selected: Set<string>` and stop firing onChoose on click — each chip toggles membership (checkmark + accent fill when on). Add a primary 'Set up my board (N)' button at the bottom plus the existing 'Skip for now'. Change the contract from `onChoose(option: ColdStartOption)` to `onChoose(options: ColdStartOption[])`. DEADLINE BAR: in src/lib/coldStart.ts add `seedColdStartMulti(options: ColdStartOption[]): { topics: Topic[]; homeItems: HomeWidgetItem[] }` that maps each option through the existing per-option topic build (worldToTopic + description + keywords, items=[]) and applies the home layout of the FIRST picked option (or a sensible merge); reuse the existing single `seedColdStart` internally so logic isn't duplicated. In Dashboard.chooseColdStart, `setTopics(prev => [...prev, ...topics])` then advance to timetable. Because 2+ topics now exist with rich keyword sets, predictTopic clears its bestScore>1 threshold from the very first capture and 'Accept all predicted' in Quicks goes live. BRIEFING BAR: extract the input-row JSX already inside DeadlineQuickAdd into the existing component but add an `alwaysOpen?: boolean` prop; when true it skips the collapsed '+ add a deadline' button and renders the full-width input row permanently. Render `<DeadlineQuickAdd ctx={ctx} accent={t.doingAccent} alwaysOpen />` at the very top of BriefingView (above the greeting/toggle header row), full-width, with the existing 'e.g. stats essay due friday 5pm' placeholder, Enter submit via the existing addDeadline path. WHY DIFFERENT: this is the minimal, surgical version — no new files, reuses parseVoiceTasks + addDeadline + the existing component, only widening two contracts and adding one alwaysOpen branch. Best if we want green TS and a shippable diff in the fewest touched lines.

variants.name: Confirm-Chip Command Bar + Live Board Preview Setup
variants.approach: Same two structural fixes, but with materially richer UX borrowed from patterns already in the codebase, so day-one feels 'alive' not just functional. COLD-START becomes a multi-select that PREVIEWS its effect: keep the Set<string> toggle model, but beside the chip grid render a tiny live 'Your board so far' strip showing the colored topic dots for each currently-selected area (pulled from worldToTopic's color), so picking Uni+Gym+Job visibly assembles her life before she commits — this directly answers the 'seeds her whole life' framing. Wire `onChoose(options[])` + `seedColdStartMulti` exactly as in Variant 1. BRIEFING BAR becomes a true command bar, not a bare input: build it by lifting the live-parse confirm-chip pattern from QuickAddWidget (lines 50-156) into the always-on bar. As she types 'stats essay friday 5pm', a memoised parseVoiceTasks(text, topics) drives an inline confirmable chip row directly under the bar showing the cleaned text + a date chip (deadlineChipLabel) AND, because cold-start seeded real topics, a predicted-topic pill (e.g. the Uni dot) before she even hits Enter. Enter routes through addDeadline (predicted topic > Deadlines bucket). This makes the highest-frequency student action a single keystroke that visibly self-files. Implement as a new small component DeadlineBar.tsx (so TodayWidget's inline DeadlineQuickAdd is untouched) consuming the same ctx; pin it at the top of BriefingView. WHY DIFFERENT: it spends a bit more code to make BOTH surfaces show their intelligence live (preview the seed, preview the parse+route), turning the fix from 'works' into 'feels smart on first run' — the dogfood-v2 'bold' bar. Slightly larger diff, one new file, but no new data model and TS stays green.

variants.name: Outcome-Goals Onboarding + Capture-First Briefing Hero
variants.approach: Keep the two code fixes as the engine but reframe the surfaces around outcomes, the boldest take. COLD-START: instead of 'What are you here for?' single chips, ask 'What are you juggling right now?' and let her toggle ALL that apply from the existing COLD_START_OPTIONS (Uni, Job hunt, Gym, Work) — multi-select with the Set<string> model and a 'Build my life board' CTA. Crucially, seed each picked area's keyword set AND, per area, drop ONE real starter deadline derived from the option (e.g. Uni -> 'add your next assignment', Job hunt -> 'follow up on an application') as a gentle, dated nudge item rather than a fake task — but make this a small, clearly-removable 'first step' so it teaches the capture loop without the placeholder-clutter the current coldStart.ts comment warns against. This requires extending seedColdStart to optionally attach one nudge item per option (guarded so the existing items=[] default still exists for skip). Wire onChoose(options[]) + multi-seed into Dashboard as before. BRIEFING: promote capture to the HERO of the surface. Restructure BriefingView so the always-on deadline bar is not just pinned above the header but is the visual focal point — a large, full-width 'What is due? (e.g. stats essay friday 5pm)' bar with the live parse chip underneath (reusing the QuickAddWidget chip pattern), and demote the greeting line to a small caption. The TodayWidget brief renders below as the 'result'. The mental model becomes: type what is due at the top, watch it land in the brief below, all on one screen — the literal 'your morning in 90 seconds, one keystroke at a time' loop. WHY DIFFERENT: this is a product reframe (juggling -> guided first deadline -> capture-first hero) not just a UI widening; it changes copy, information hierarchy, and adds one opinionated nudge item per area. Highest upside for day-one momentum, largest surface-area change, still fully self-contained React/TS with no auth/payments/schema touch.

COLD-START: the live "Your board so far" dot strip pulls each selected area's color from worldToTopic and literally assembles my life as I tap Uni+Gym+Job, directly delivering the "seeds her whole life" promise instead of just toggling chips blind. BRIEFING: it lifts the already-proven confirm-chip pattern from QuickAddWidget (lines 50-156) into an always-on bar, so typing "stats essay friday 5pm" shows the cleaned text + date chip + a predicted-topic pill BEFORE I hit Enter, then routes via addDeadline (predicted topic > Deadlines bucket). That is the single-keystroke, visibly-self-filing loop a power user wants; Variant 1's alwaysOpen DeadlineQuickAdd is a bare input with no live parse and no topic pill, which actually feels dumber than the QuickAdd widget I already have. I rejected Variant 3 despite its upside because it re-introduces per-area "starter deadline" nudge items, the exact placeholder-clutter coldStart.ts:11-15 deliberately removed ("friction, not a head start"), and it restructures the whole BriefingView hierarchy/greeting, which is a large blast radius against the 90-second glance I open the app for. Variant 2 is implemented as a new self-contained DeadlineBar.tsx (TodayWidget's inline DeadlineQuickAdd untouched), adds no new data model, never touches auth/payments/schema, and keeps TS green: the bold-but-surgical choice.

changes.title: Type-your-timetable front door: add a default 'Build / type my classes' tab to WelcomeTimetable (plain-English line parser), demote .ics paste to a secondary tab
changes.approach: Restructure WelcomeTimetable into a two-tab overlay with 'Type my classes' selected by default and 'Paste a link (.ics)' second (the existing AddFeedForm block moves under the second tab unchanged). Build the default tab as a multiline <textarea> where each line is one class, plus a tiny weekday-letter row (M T W T F S S, reusing the exact toggle UI from CalendarView's CreateEventForm lines 156-201) and two <input type=date> term-start/term-end fields defaulting to today -> +16wk (same defaults as CreateEventForm lines 80-81). Add a pure parser `parseClassLines(text): Omit<CalendarNote,'id'|'color'>[]` in src/lib/calendar.ts (no new file, sits beside noteEvents): regex over each line to pull weekday tokens (mon/tue/.../weekend names + abbrevs -> Date.getDay() 0-6), a time range (`9-11`, `9:00-11:00`, `9am-11am` -> startMin/endMin), a trailing room/`Room X` -> location, and the remainder as title; lines with a weekday+time become repeat:{weekdays,termStart,termEnd}; date-less lines are skipped with a soft inline count ('2 lines need a day + time'). Render a live preview list of parsed classes below the box (title - weekday(s) - HH:MM, colour swatch cycling appearance.colorBank) so it confirms before commit. On 'Add these classes', map each parsed class to a CalendarNote (id = Date.now()+rand, color from colorBank cycling, date = localMidnight(termStart)) and push via a new onAddClasses callback wired in Dashboard to setCalendarNotes(prev => [...prev, ...notes]) then finishWelcome(). Keep onSkip/finishWelcome and the help popover. Update the WelcomeTimetable props to add onAddClasses and pass calendarNotes-free (notes are append-only).
changes.risk: Medium - the line parser is the only genuinely new logic; it is pure and unit-testable, feeds the already-shipped noteEvents() expansion verified in lib/calendar.ts (lines 68-90) and the CalendarEvent shape TodayWidget already renders, so a parse miss degrades to 'line skipped', never a crash. No schema/network/auth touched; .ics path is preserved verbatim under tab 2.

changes.title: First-class Deadlines hub as a new top-level section: the Triage Board (Overdue / This week / This month / Later columns) with module-colour filter chips, inline complete, and an always-on inline add
changes.approach: Add 'deadlines' to the SectionId union in types.ts, add { id:'deadlines', label:'Deadlines', icon: Flame } to allSections (Dashboard.tsx ~line 896-904, import Flame from lucide-react), and render `{activeSection === 'deadlines' && <DeadlinesView .../>}` next to the calendar block (~line 1588). New DeadlinesView.tsx builds its own WidgetCtx-shaped object (same fields already assembled for BriefingView at lines 1540-1548, including topics, upcomingEvents, addDeadline, onAdvanceStage, colorBank, setActiveSection) and calls deadlineEntries(ctx) from widgets/util.ts to get the unified DeadlineEntry[] (topic items + deduped calendar deadlines). Bucket each entry by dueTimestamp(item) vs now into four urgency columns (overdue<now, this week <=+7d, this month <=+~30d, later) rendered as four vertical lanes (mobile: stacked). Each card shows item.text, a colour dot in entry.accent, the relative due label, and a click that calls ctx.setActiveSection(entry.section) to jump to its topic/calendar. Inline complete: a checkbox that, for topic-backed entries (positive item.id), calls ctx.onAdvanceStage(entry.section, entry.id) repeatedly to reach the done stage (or add a lighter onCompleteItem; reuse onAdvanceStage to stay zero-new-state); calendar-derived entries (negative synthetic id from eventItemId) render read-only with a 'from calendar' tag. Module filter chips at the top: derive the distinct {section,accent,name} set from the entries and toggle a visible Set<sectionId>; chips reuse the pill style from CalendarView DayPanel (lines 757-777). Put a full-width DeadlineQuickAdd (existing component, forced open) at the top so add-from-here works on a zero-topic account.
changes.risk: Low-Medium - pure composition over deadlineEntries()/dueTimestamp() (util.ts) and existing ctx callbacks; the only subtlety is inline-complete only applying to topic items, which the negative-vs-positive id convention in util.ts (eventItemId, lines 27-31) makes a clean guard. New SectionId member needs the same string added anywhere SectionId is exhaustively switched - grep before commit.

changes.title: Lift the 14-day upcomingEvents cap to a term-length window so the Deadlines hub 'Later' column actually spans the term
changes.approach: upcomingEvents (Dashboard.tsx lines 867-873) currently filters feed+note events to [today, today+14d], which would truncate the new 'Later' column. Widen the window to ~120 days (one term) by changing the `to` bound from 14 to ~120 days. Keep the lower bound and the dedupe contract identical so Today Priorities / Upcoming-deadlines widgets are unaffected in their own slicing. noteEvents() already self-bounds recurring classes to RECUR_WINDOW_DAYS=30 (lib/calendar.ts line 6) so the recurring-class volume stays capped; only one-off notes + feed events extend, which is exactly what the hub needs. No other call site changes.
changes.risk: Low - single constant change on a memoized derived array; widgets that consume upcomingEvents already do their own date filtering, so a wider source set cannot regress them. Worst case is more events in memory (bounded by feed size).

changes.title: Multi-select cold-start so day one seeds her whole life (Uni + Gym + Job) and makes prediction fire from the first capture
changes.approach: Convert WelcomeColdStart from immediate single onChoose to multi-select: hold a local Set<string> of chosen option ids, render each chip with a selected (checked) visual state instead of firing on click, and add a primary 'Set up my board' button that calls a new onChooseMany(options: ColdStartOption[]). Add `seedColdStartMany(options): { topics: Topic[]; homeItems: HomeWidgetItem[] }` in coldStart.ts that maps each option through the existing seedColdStart logic (worldToTopic + description + keywords, lines 114-124) assigning order 0..n and distinct colours, and picks the home layout of the FIRST chosen option (or merges - keep it simple: first option's starterHome). In Dashboard, replace chooseColdStart with a chooseColdStartMany that setTopics(prev => [...prev, ...seeded.topics]) and setHomeItems(seeded.homeItems), then advances to 'timetable'. Because predictTopic requires bestScore>1 AND >=1 topic (taskParser.ts lines 262-271) and addDeadline routes via predictTopic (Dashboard line 770), seeding 2-3 keyword-rich topics at once makes routing + Quicks 'Accept all predicted' live from the very first capture. Keep onSkip intact (skips to timetable, no topics).
changes.risk: Low - additive, brand-new-account-only (gated by welcomePhase like the current path). Reuses the existing single-seed machinery per option; the only new surface is the selection state and a thin many-wrapper. Topic colours must stay distinct - cycle appearance.colorBank when assigning.

changes.title: Promote DeadlineQuickAdd to an always-on confirm-chip command bar pinned at the top of the Briefing
changes.approach: Add an `alwaysOpen?: boolean` prop to DeadlineQuickAdd: when set, skip the collapsed '+ add a deadline' button (lines 42-56) and render the input row full-width by default, never collapsing on blur. Enhance submit to the confirm-chip pattern: on Enter, run parseVoiceTasks(raw, ctx.topics) (it already extracts + strips the date and predicts a topic), and instead of committing immediately, show a confirmable chip row beneath the input - the cleaned text, a date chip (parsed.deadlineLabel or 'no date'), and the predicted topic name/colour - with an Enter-again / click-to-confirm that calls ctx.addDeadline(clean, deadline) and clears; Escape discards the pending chip. In BriefingView, render <DeadlineQuickAdd ctx={briefingCtx} accent={t.doingAccent} alwaysOpen /> as a pinned full-width bar directly under the greeting/toggle header (before the TodayWidget block at line 78), inside a subtle bordered container so it reads as the primary capture affordance. briefingCtx already carries addDeadline + topics.
changes.risk: Low - DeadlineQuickAdd already owns the parse+addDeadline path (lines 28-40); this adds an alwaysOpen branch and a pending-confirm state without changing the default collapsed behaviour used inside Today/Upcoming widgets, so those call sites are untouched. addDeadline is optional in ctx but present in briefingCtx.

plan.summary: Five grounded, bold-but-safe changes that deliver the round's three agenda priorities end to end, all pure React/TS over already-shipped state (no auth/payments/secrets/schema/CI). (1) The headline unblock: WelcomeTimetable gets a default 'type my classes' tab with a plain-English line parser feeding the existing CalendarNote.repeat -> noteEvents() pipeline, demoting .ics to a secondary tab - routing a non-techy student to a populated Today at setup. (2) A first-class Deadlines section rendered as the chosen Triage Board (urgency columns, module-colour chips, inline complete, inline add) composed purely over deadlineEntries()/dueTimestamp(), the clean domino after activation. (3) A one-line cap lift on upcomingEvents (14d -> term) so the hub's 'Later' column spans the term. (4) Multi-select cold-start (seedColdStartMany) so day one seeds Uni+Gym+Job at once, making predictTopic and Quicks 'Accept all' live from the first capture. (5) DeadlineQuickAdd promoted to an always-on confirm-chip command bar pinned atop the Briefing. Each cites the exact files and existing seams verified by reading them; risk is concentrated only in the new pure line-parser, which degrades gracefully to skipped lines.
